free_text: Elastic scattering cross sections of 11.4  MeV
obtained from netron capture  in  59Ni  an  unstable
isotope   with  a  half-life  75000  yr,  which  was
produced by neutron capture on the stable (and  most
of 5 kg of natural Ni (in the form of  six  separate
disks) was placed in a tangential tube near the core
Monoenergetic photons of 11.4 MeV,  originating  from
the  Ni(n,g) rection were elastically scattered from
three isotopically enriched lead  targets,  a  209Bi
target, and  a 181Ta target.  Cross sections for the
lead isotopes at theta = 90 and  140  degree  differ
significantly  from predictions based on known giant
strucrtures  in  this  region  of  enery.  The cross
sections for 209Bi and 181Ta are in agreement with a
previous measurement. The 181Ta cross section is the
only case where agreement is found with calculations
based  on  known GDR parameters.  New GDR parameters
for 209Bi were  deduced  by  combining  the  present
elastic   scattering   with   those   from  previous
Data  from  Table  2 were prepared at the MSU
